volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Casina (Section 5.4.996 - Section 5.4.1015)

sections.label: Section 5.4.996
sections.original: te quidem oppresset .
sections.translation: He ’ d have been making an attack upon you , in fact .

sections.label: Section 5.4.996b
sections.original: Feci égo istaec dicta quae vos dicitis ?
sections.translation: I been doing these things that you mention ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.997
sections.original: Rogitas etiam ?
sections.translation: And do you ask me that ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.997b
sections.original: Si quidem hercle feci , feci nequiter .
sections.translation: If indeed I have done so , I ’ ve been doing wrong .

sections.label: Section 5.4.998
sections.original: Redi modo huc intro : monebo , si qui meministi minus .
sections.translation: Just come back in-doors here ; I ’ ll remind you , if you have forgotten anything .

sections.label: Section 5.4.999
sections.original: Hercle , opinor , potius vobis credam quod vos dicitis .
sections.translation: Troth , I think , I ’ ll believe you in preference as to what you say .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1000
sections.original: sed , uxor , da viro tuo hanc veniam . Myrrhina , ora Cleostratam ;
sections.translation: But , wife , do grant pardon to your husband for this ; Myrrhina , do entreat Cleostrata ! If ever , from this time forward , I love Casina , or even think of it , should I love her , I say , should I ever hereafter , in fact , be guilty of such a thing , there ’ s no reason , wife , why you shouldn ’ t lash me with twigs as I hang up by the arms .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1004
sections.original: Censeo ecastor veniam hanc dandam .
sections.translation: On my word , I do think that forgiveness may be granted for this .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1004b
sections.original: Faciam ut iubes .
sections.translation: I ’ ll do as you request me .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1005
sections.original: propter eam rem hanc tibi nunc veniam minus gravate prospero ,
sections.translation: On this account with the less difficulty do I now grant you this pardon , that , from being a long one , we mayn ’ t be making this Play still longer .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1007
sections.original: Non irata es ?
sections.translation: You are not angry ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.1007b
sections.original: Non sum irata .
sections.translation: I am not angry .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1007c
sections.original: Tuaen fidei credo ?
sections.translation: Am I to trust your word ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.1007d
sections.original: Meae .
sections.translation: You may my word .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1008
sections.original: Lepidiorem uxorem nemo quisquam quam ego habeo hanc habet .
sections.translation: No person ever did have a more amiable wife than I ’ ve got .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1009
sections.original: Age tu , redde huic scipionem et pallium .
sections.translation: Keep to her , then .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1009b
sections.original: Tene , si lubet .
sections.translation: Take them , if you wish .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1010
sections.original: mihi quidem edepol insignite factast magna iniuria :
sections.translation: Upon my faith , a great injustice has really been most egregiously done me ; I ’ ve been married to two husbands ; neither has behaved to me as to a new-made bride .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1012
sections.original: Spectatores , quod futurumst intus , id memorabimus .
sections.translation: Spectators , what ’ s to be done within , we ’ ll tell you here . This Casina will be discovered to be the daughter of this person next door [*] , and she ’ ll be married to Euthynicus , our master ’ s son .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1015
sections.original: nunc vos aequomst manibus meritis meritam mercedem dare :
sections.translation: Now it ’ s only fair that with your deserving hands you should give us deserved applause . He who does so , may he always keep his mistress without the knowledge of his wife . But he who doesn ’ t with his hands clap as loud as he can , in place of a mistress , may a he-goat , soused in bilge-water , be palmed off upon him [*] .
